description_long: This object is a short sword with a broad, double-edged blade that tapers to a point, reminiscent of a Roman gladius. The blade is dark in color, likely made of metal, and has a smooth, slightly convex profile along its length. The hilt features a straight crossguard and a cylindrical handle, which appears to be wrapped or textured for grip, ending in a rounded pommel. The handle and pommel are a deep, reddish-brown color, suggesting a wood or leather material. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or engravings. The proportions and construction indicate it is intended for one-handed use, suitable for close combat or ceremonial display.
description: A short, double-edged sword with a dark metal blade and a reddish-brown cylindrical handle, featuring a straight crossguard and rounded pommel. The design is simple and functional, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the broad, double-edged blade and the straight crossguard with the cylindrical handle and rounded pommel clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the blade, emphasizing its straightness and the cylindrical shape of the handle and pommel.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again showing the broad blade, crossguard, and handle, with no additional features visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to view 1, highlighting the slim edge of the blade and the handle's cylindrical form.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, dark reddish-brown, rounded pommel, likely wood or leather-wrapped
